route.name: Lost Canyon Overnighter
route.description: Public GPX short overnighter with mesa roads, primitive camping, and dry-country water decisions.
route.surfaceMix: Public GPX short overnighter with mesa roads, primitive camping, and dry-country water decisions.
route.bestSeason: Typically melts out by June; July works with mud, heat, and storm checks.

difficultyProfile.surface: Public GPX short overnighter with mesa roads, primitive camping, and dry-country water decisions.
difficultyProfile.traffic: review paved connectors and trailhead access
difficultyProfile.technical: moderate unless source notes say otherwise

resources.water: Beaver Creek is reliable; Lost Canyon Gulch is not. Filter due cattle.
resources.food: No food resupply on route; full services in Gunnison before/after.
resources.camping: Primitive camps are usually available; final camp choice needs legal and condition confirmation.
resources.toiletsShowers: Use mapped waypoints and land-manager pages; confirm current operation before departure.
resources.bikeShops: Identify nearest shops and exact hours during final trip refresh.
resources.longestGap: Measure from imported GPX and source POIs before gate confirmation.
resources.mosquitoes: Seasonal; check recent reports for camp and water-adjacent sections.

tips: Good short Gunnison option after weather check.
Avoid if Signal Mesa is wet.

verification.notes: Public GPX imported and parsed locally. Final legal/resource checks still control gate confirmation.

mapQuality.reviewRule: Route line is imported from public GPX and suitable for planning review.

review.decision: promote to plan_ready source coverage only; gate status remains blocked until date-specific gates pass
blockers: Not gate-confirmed until date-specific camp/permit, water, legal access, closures, fire/smoke, and weather are refreshed.
Best candidate by distance/elevation, but still lacks exact verified legal campsite coordinates.
Signal Mesa/Lost Canyon mud status not refreshed; monsoonal moisture can make soils unrideable.
Active BLM/USFS Stage 2 fire restrictions require stove-only/no campfire plan.

requiredFixes: Complete July 9-10 final refresh before next-weekend gate confirmation.
Pick exact camp coordinate near One Mile/Beaver Creek or Signal Mesa and verify BLM/USFS/public-land status plus no posted camping closure.
Check Gunnison Trails/COTREX/BLM Gunnison Field Office for Signal Peak and Lost Canyon Road conditions after any rain.
Check BLM Southwest District and GMUG alerts on July 9-10.
Refresh NWS Gunnison/Signal Peak forecast within 24-48 hours and cancel if storms make clay soils unrideable.
Refresh smoke/fire dashboards immediately before departure.
Obtain Gunnison Ranger District and/or BLM Gunnison Field Office confirmation for exact route access and selected camp coordinates.
Select one exact legal camp coordinate and document public-land status plus fire/stay restrictions.
Refresh GMUG/BLM alerts, InciWeb/Watch Duty, AirNow smoke, and NWS point forecast on July 9 or July 10.
Verify Signal Mesa/Signal Peak trail conditions after any rain and cancel if clay/mud is active.
Confirm Beaver Creek flow or require a full dry-camp water carry.
For Gate Confirmed, overlay the GPX against official access and closure sources.
For Gate Confirmed, choose exact overnight, water/resupply, bailout, and parking plan.
For Gate Confirmed, refresh weather, smoke, fire, and road/park alerts close to departure.
openQuestions: Do current closures/fire/weather alter the imported GPX?

promotionChecklist.targetWindowNote: Short Sat-Sun Gunnison option if Signal Mesa is dry and heat/storms are acceptable.
promotionChecklist.officialGeometryNote: Official/public GPX has been imported and parsed locally.

days.name: Lost Canyon Overnighter segment 1
days.camp: Primitive camps are usually available; final camp choice needs legal and condition confirmation.

logistics: Final gate confirmation requires live date-specific resource and legal checks.

readinessEvidence.finding: Bikepacking.com publishes the route and downloadable GPX; local GPX/GeoJSON artifacts exist.

The loop uses county roads, BLM land, and national forest, but exact online land-manager proof for every segment was not found; GMUG directs users to contact Gunnison Ranger District for current road conditions.
readinessEvidence.sourceName: GMUG road conditions and route guide

readinessEvidence.finding: Primitive camp waypoints near One Mile/Beaver Creek and Signal Mesa are present, but exact July 11 legal camping at those coordinates is not proven by official source.

vettingResults.decision: near miss but not gate_confirmed; best Colorado candidate by distance, but exact legal campsite, mud/monsoon condition, and Stage 2 fire/weather refresh are missing
